title: Update vendor of containers/common to v0.6.1
body:

comments.body: @lsm5 @TomSweeneyRedHat @QiWang19 @vrothberg PTAL

Can you tweak the title from 1.6.1 to 0.6.1 please?
LGTM otherwise
This should fix the compiler problem @lsm5 saw in rawhide.
LGTM
bors r+
:clock1: Waiting for PR status (Github check) to be set, probably by CI. Bors will automatically try to run when all required PR statuses are set.
bors r+

# Build failed
  * cirrus-ci/success
bors retry

# Build succeeded
  * cirrus-ci/success
@edsantiago Is buildah building in Rawhide again?

It [doesn't look like it](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/packageinfo?packageID=24131). Smells like another 32-bit problem. [logs of latest build attempt this morning](https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/taskinfo?taskID=42611322)

@edsantiago, do build break frequently enough on Rawhide to justify a (non-blocking) job in the usptream CI here? We could run builds in a rawhide container. Maybe there's already such a pipeline that I may not be aware of.

Builds break constantly, but it's nothing to do with rawhide, and everything to do with 32-bit incompatibilities. We catch them in rawhide because there are frequent builds. I believe the CI setup should include `make` on a 32-bit system.
Right we should be compiling Podman with a 32 bit compiler.
Something like https://github.com/containers/common/pull/94
still failing https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/taskinfo?taskID=42618824
err, my buildah job might not be updating atm, ignore ^
Not easy to see what is going wrong?
the autobuild job wasn't updating the commit, so it was building an older version, doing a new one now..

builds fine now https://koji.fedoraproject.org/koji/taskinfo?taskID=42619477
Thanks.
